title: Concept Sets
description: Concept Sets are reusable collections of OMOP standard vocabulary concepts that define clinical phenomena such as conditions, drugs, procedures, measurements, and observations. They serve as the building blocks for cohort definitions, analyses, and phenotype libraries across the Parthenon platform.
tips: Use the Vocabulary Browser to explore concept hierarchies before finalizing your concept set — including descendants of a high-level concept often captures more complete case ascertainment.
The Mapping Assistant can help translate source codes (ICD-10, CPT, SNOMED) to their OMOP standard equivalents when building concept sets from existing clinical code lists.
Exclude concepts that represent rule-out or family-history contexts by using the 'Exclude' flag on specific concept set items.
Save and version concept sets independently of the cohort definitions that use them so that a single update propagates to all dependent analyses.
Annotate concept sets with clinical rationale and curation date so that future reviewers understand the intent and currency of the included concepts.
